message:
feat(harness): add kernel snapshot capture/diff commands, asupersync orchestrator, fix mode=both execution

Three new harness modules and two new CLI subcommands for deterministic
runtime_math kernel snapshot regression testing:

- kernel_snapshot.rs: Deterministic snapshot capture that drives a
  RuntimeMathKernel through a fixed sequence of decide/observe cycles
  using a seeded PRNG. Produces a RuntimeKernelSnapshotFixtureV1 JSON
  fixture with scenario metadata (id, seed, steps, families) and per-mode
  (strict/hardened) snapshots. No timestamps — output is fully reproducible.
  The fixture schema version is pinned to match RUNTIME_KERNEL_SNAPSHOT_
  SCHEMA_VERSION. SnapshotMode enum supports Strict/Hardened/Both.

- snapshot_diff.rs: Side-by-side diff rendering for two kernel snapshot
  fixtures. Compares golden vs current field-by-field with delta computation
  and status classification (Same/Changed/Alert based on configurable
  thresholds). Key fields (decisions, consistency_faults, risk triggers,
  pareto metrics, quarantine depth, etc.) are always shown; --all flag
  includes the full field set. Supports two render backends:
  - render_plain(): ASCII table for CI logs.
  - render_ftui(): FrankentUI-powered ANSI table (behind frankentui-ui
    feature gate) with color-coded status columns.

- asupersync_orchestrator.rs: Deterministic fixture verification campaign
  runner behind the asupersync-tooling feature gate. Provides stable
  ordering (fixture sets sorted by family/timestamp/version, cases sorted
  by function/name/mode), traceability IDs, and structured evidence
  capture via asupersync_conformance checkpoints. Handles mode=both cases
  by executing under both strict and hardened runners. Emits suite.json
  alongside the existing markdown/json reports.

Harness binary (harness.rs) changes:
- New snapshot-kernel subcommand: captures deterministic kernel snapshots
  to a JSON fixture file. Accepts --output, --mode (strict|hardened|both),
  --seed (decimal or 0x hex with underscores), --steps.
- New diff-kernel-snapshot subcommand: diffs golden vs current fixtures
  with optional ANSI rendering. Accepts --golden, --current, --mode,
  --all, --ansi, --width. If --current file is missing, generates one
  in-memory from the golden scenario parameters.
- Verify subcommand refactored: fixture paths now sorted for deterministic
  ordering. When asupersync-tooling is enabled, delegates to the new
  orchestrator and writes an additional suite.json report.
- parse_seed() utility: handles decimal and 0x-prefixed hex with optional
  underscore separators.

Runner (runner.rs) fixes:
- mode=both fixtures now execute under the runner's active mode instead of
  passing "both" to execute_fixture_case, which was incorrect. The case
  name is annotated with [strict] or [hardened] to distinguish results.
- Host parity check now uses the active execution mode, not the fixture's
  declared mode.
- New test: both_mode_fixture_executes_under_active_mode validates the fix.

Cargo.toml changes:
- frankentui-ui feature expanded to include ftui-core, ftui-layout,
  ftui-render, ftui-style, ftui-widgets dependencies for the snapshot
  diff UI.
- glibc-rs-membrane added as a dependency (needed by kernel_snapshot module).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
